title: Implement isolated browser execution

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.expectedFailure: All three focused suites failed to import the not-yet-created isolated-browser runner module

commands.expectedFailure: The first implementation had an incomplete trace-schema closure and failed during source transformation

Curated trace privacy scan

commands.expectedFailure: The evidence inventory still expected 148 proof files after the two source-bound RUN-01 traces became the 149th and 150th unique proofs

scenarios.scenario: Sequential authorized runs receive separate local browser resources
A crashed renderer releases its run before clean recovery

visualEvidence.rationale: Machine-readable lifecycle traces are applicable and required. A screenshot is not applicable because RUN-01 adds no visible UI or isolation-error surface.

assets.altText: Machine-readable lifecycle trace for two sequential real-Chromium runs, showing terminal cleanup for the first run and empty cookie, local-storage, clipboard, and prior-marker assertions for the second run.
assets.caption: The first run retained its isolated download only through artifact finalization, released its browser and temporary root, and the second run at the same authorized origin observed no browser-local state from it.

assets.altText: Machine-readable lifecycle trace for a real Chromium renderer crash followed by a recovery run, showing crash cleanup and empty browser-local state in recovery.
assets.caption: A CDP-induced renderer crash produced a CRASHED terminal session, removed its isolated download and temporary root, disconnected its browser, and allowed a clean recovery run.

proofs.description: Strict session policy, permanent resource registry, per-run Chromium lifecycle, lower-layer request and WebSocket gates, isolated local capabilities, cleanup, diagnostics, and sanitized trace schema
Exact-origin and host policy, URL-credential and protocol refusal, strict configuration, and permanent resource non-reuse examples
Seeded PROP-20 run-order, concurrency, release, and permanent non-reassignment invariants
Real Chromium sequential and concurrent isolation, HTTP/WebSocket egress, popup, clipboard, download, renderer-crash, cleanup, and recovery checks
User-visible sequential isolation story tagged RUN-01, FR-030, and PROP-20
User-visible renderer crash and clean recovery story tagged RUN-01, FR-030, and PROP-20
Controlled fictional fixture, real per-run Chromium resources, crash injection, terminal-resource assertions, and sanitized trace capture
Source-bound optimized-production trace proving sequential finalization, resource destruction, and absence of prior browser-local state
Source-bound optimized-production trace proving renderer-crash cleanup and a clean recovery run

knownLimitations: RUN-01 exposes the isolated execution primitive to trusted runner code; RUN-03 still owns the policy-bounded GPT-5.6 computer-use adapter and ensures the model never receives raw Playwright authority.
RUN-02 still owns independent deterministic observation, capture-gap classification, and recorder-version evidence.

The network boundary is host based and enforced below future model code at the Playwright HTTP and WebSocket routing layers; this task does not claim an operating-system container or firewall boundary.
No UI screenshot is applicable because RUN-01 adds no visible UI or isolation-error surface; its required evidence is a machine-readable lifecycle trace.
All exercised hosts, identities, markers, and downloads are controlled fictional fixtures; the passing cases do not establish safety, compliance, agreement conformity, or product effectiveness.
